response:
Section 1  
I need to write a Python function that counts the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The function should take two inputs: `text` (a string) and `word` (a string). The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.

Section 2  
To handle the case insensitivity, I will convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase (or uppercase) before comparing them. This ensures that "Freedom" and "freedom" are treated as the same word. I will also split the `text` into words using the `split()` method, which splits on whitespace and punctuation.

Section 3  
Next, I will iterate through the list of words and count how many times the `word` appears. I will use a `count` variable to keep track of the occurrences. Since the function should be case-insensitive, I will convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase before comparison.

Section 4  
Finally, I will return the count of the specified word in the text. The function will be structured to handle all the requirements, including case insensitivity and handling punctuation.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections.
